(4) This paragraph applies to—


(5) A person to whom paragraph (4) does not apply may be provided with a transcript of the recording of any hearing—


(6) At any hearing, the court may give appropriate directions to assist a party, in particular one who is or has been or may become unrepresented, for the compilation and sharing of a note or other informal record of the proceedings made by another party. ]


Textual Amendments


F14 Rule 27.9 substituted (6.4.2020) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/135) , rules 1 , 28


F15 Word in rule 27.9(4)(b) substituted (6.4.2023) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2023 (S.I. 2023/61) , rules 1(3) , 11


### Hearings in private E+W


27.10. —(1) Proceedings to which these rules apply will be held in private, except—


(2) For the purposes of these rules, a reference to proceedings held “ in private ” means proceedings at which the general public have no right to be present.


### Attendance at private hearings E+W


27.11. —(1) This rule applies when proceedings are held in private, except in relation to —


(2) When this rule applies, no person shall be present during any hearing other than—


[ F20 (Practice Direction 27C makes provision for the attendance at hearings of Independent Domestic Violence Advisers and Independent Sexual Violence Advisers.) ]


(3) At any stage of the proceedings the court may direct that persons within paragraph (2)(f) [ F21 and (ff) ] shall not attend the proceedings or any part of them, where satisfied that—


(4) The court may exercise the power in paragraph (3) of its own initiative or pursuant to representations made by any of the persons listed in paragraph (5), and in either case having given to any person within paragraph (2)(f) [ F22 or (ff) ] who is in attendance an opportunity to make representations.
